description:
Define the Participant trait and land git-remote sync as its canonical implementation, exercising the bl-eae4 negotiation primitive.

## Trait surface (per bl-26de SPEC)

A Participant declares:
- Event subscriptions: which lifecycle events it participates in (claim, review, close, sync, on-update).
- Field projection: which Task fields it owns authoritatively, which it reads, the merge semantics over the overlap.
- Failure policy: required / best-effort / gating, per event, defaultable.
- Protocol implementation: the wire layer the negotiation primitive drives.

## Reference implementation: git-remote

The state-branch sync (bl-2148) becomes the canonical Participant. After this ball lands, the claim-side sync code path goes through Participant -> Negotiation rather than the inlined loop bl-2148 introduced.

## Out of scope for this ball

- Existing balls-plugin-{name} subprocess plugins are NOT yet rewired. They keep firing through the legacy dispatcher in commands/lifecycle.rs. The shim that bridges them onto Participants is a separate ball (next).
- Review/close lifecycle hooks are a separate ball.
- New plugin protocol features (declarative projection, conflict reporting) are a separate ball.

## Tests

End-to-end claim sync tests continue to pass. New tests cover the trait surface with a fake Participant: subscription dispatch, projection-restricted merge, failure-policy branching.
title: Participant contract and git-remote reference implementation
